## [00:00 - 03:02]

Okay, so the number one core strategy I want to cover here, and the deal is this would be my number one core strategy, no matter what product, what I was teaching you in terms of business, and that is the list. It's not going to do any good until you have 1,000 people, or 10,000 people, or 30,000 people, or 100,000 people. Get that out of your mind. Everyone starts with zero subscribers and they build it from there. The thing is, small lists are strong. Your results, your conversions from the first thousand people are going to be strong. I've seen many, many times in launches that small lists outperform much bigger lists. So it isn't like a 1,000 person list is going to outperform a 100,000 person list, but a 1,000 person list is on a per name basis, on a per subscriber basis, is generally going to get much better results than that 100,000 person list. You need three things. You need a squeeze page, you need an opt-in bribe, what we call an ethical bribe, and you need to put some kind of traffic to that squeeze page. So the squeeze page with the ethical bribe is like, join my list and I'll give you this thing. when people opt-in to get their bribe, they're building your list. ## [06:03 - 07:51]

Now here's the key most important thing about lists is the actual list and the size of the list is not very important. It's your relationship to the list. Do they open your emails? Do they do whatever you tell them to do? Do they click on your links? I've got all kinds of insight from being on the insides of many, many, many launches, including big joint venture launches. I've seen this over and over where people with huge lists that don't perform, other people with small lists that do perform, and it's all about that list relationship. Again, I'm going to go way deep on this in the Launching Your List course, but relationship trumps list size. So, and really, you know, one of the things is the best relationship. This is sort of counter to the best relationship comes from doing a launch. Like a lot of times people think, if I do a launch, I send people a whole bunch of pre-launch content, all this email about my launch, they're going to hate me. The reality is if you're putting out good content and you have a good sideways sales ladder, and that sideways sales ladder, you know, leads people through the sequence that opportunity transformation, ownership experience, and really teaches and shares throughout the experience and tells a good story over that whole launch story arc, then people are going to love you. They actually, that's how when you have the absolute warmest list is after the launch, or the day after the launch ends when you have the warmest list. Both the people who buy from you and the people who haven't bought from you, you know, they're just still in your prospect list, they're going to love you. So, launches are a great way to build that launch relationship. And remember, like I said, no one starts off with a list. I didn't start off with a list. And when I changed markets, and I've changed markets several times, I didn't have a list. So, each time I just started over and started building that list. So, this is a case of, you know what, baby steps.
